what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG plus icon drawn with two stroked line segments using the current text color.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
The icon is stroke-based with fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
The plus shape is a single path made from a vertical segment and a horizontal segment.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
standouts: Compact inline SVG with no metadata or embedded styling.
conventions: Follows a common 24px icon grid convention.
Uses stroke attributes directly on the SVG root.
Uses currentColor for theme-compatible icon coloring.
Does not include title, role, aria, or descriptive metadata in the file.
concerns: No accessible name or aria-hidden marker is present, so standalone semantics depend on the consumer.
notes: A minimal theme-colored plus icon asset for add/create style affordances.
questions: Should accessibility semantics be supplied by the consuming component or embedded in the asset?
Is the fixed 24px width and height intended to be overridden by CSS when needed?
purpose_vs_actual: The path and filename indicate a plus icon, and the SVG content draws a plus sign.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable plus symbol that adapts to surrounding text or icon color.
contribution: Contributes a compact, reusable plus glyph with currentColor theming and simple stroke geometry for add/create visual language in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px stroke-based SVG plus icon using currentColor and a single path for vertical and horizontal strokes.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing an additive or create action as a reusable themed icon asset.
